description: Hereditary haemochromatosis HFE risk-genotype panel. Directly-typed C282Y (rs1800562) and H63D (rs1799945). Genotype-combination-specific calls with sex-stratified penetrance. Treatable by phlebotomy — high value. EXPANSION_STRATEGY.md §6 / roadmap #23.

by_sex.XY: In men, cumulative haemochromatosis diagnosis reached 56.4% by age 80 (liver disease ~20.3%).
by_sex.XX: In women, cumulative haemochromatosis diagnosis reached 40.5% by age 80 (liver disease ~8.3%).

genotype_models.finding_text: HFE C282Y homozygous ({genotype}) — the genotype most strongly associated with hereditary haemochromatosis (iron overload). {penetrance_text} Penetrance is reduced and sex-dependent, and most C282Y homozygotes are undiagnosed (~88% of men, ~97% of women at enrolment in UK Biobank). The condition is treatable by phlebotomy; discuss ferritin and transferrin-saturation testing with a clinician.

genotype_models.penetrance: Compound heterozygotes (one C282Y, one H63D) have low penetrance; most never develop clinically significant iron overload.
genotype_models.finding_text: HFE compound heterozygous C282Y/H63D ({genotype}). {penetrance_text} This is a low-penetrance genotype — the large majority of compound heterozygotes do not develop iron-overload disease. Routine iron studies may be discussed with a clinician.

genotype_models.penetrance: A single C282Y allele carries low risk of iron overload on its own.
genotype_models.finding_text: HFE C282Y heterozygous ({genotype}) — you carry one C282Y allele. {penetrance_text} Single C282Y carriers are typically unaffected; the genotype is mainly relevant for family planning and when combined with a second HFE variant.

genotype_models.penetrance: H63D homozygosity is of low clinical significance and rarely causes iron overload by itself.
genotype_models.finding_text: HFE H63D homozygous ({genotype}). {penetrance_text} This genotype is of limited clinical significance in isolation.

genotype_models.penetrance: A single H63D allele is of minimal clinical significance.
genotype_models.finding_text: HFE H63D heterozygous ({genotype}) — you carry one H63D allele. {penetrance_text} Mainly relevant for family planning or in combination with a C282Y allele.
